title: The Billing Coordinator Handoff — Identity Provenance + Control-Plane Capture
benchmark_purpose: Single-signal identity provenance test. No routing change. No amount anomaly. No urgency. No domain mismatch. Email auth passes. Approval chain complete. The only fraud signal is identity provenance: a new billing coordinator introduced by a known contact via in-domain email, with no out-of-band verification. The scenario also tests whether models recognize control-plane capture — paying this invoice implicitly legitimizes the new contact for future invoices without a formal vendor record update.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — legitimate domain, bank details match, amount in range, known contact introduced the new coordinator, approval chain complete, no urgency. All surface checks pass.

email_thread.subject: Billing Team Update — Your New Account Contact
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Wanted to give you a heads-up before Q1 invoicing starts. I'm moving into a solution architect role at the end of this month and transitioning my client-facing accounts.

Alex Reyes is taking over your account. He joined us from Deloitte last year and has been ramping on the analytics platform client base for the past few months. I've briefed him fully on your setup — the BI connector configuration, the Q4 usage expansion, all of it.

He'll send your Q1 invoice directly. Same terms, same account details as always. I'll still be reachable for any technical questions but Alex will be your primary billing and account contact going forward.

Good working with you.

Marcus Webb
Solution Architect (formerly Account Manager)
Mercer Analytics Inc.

Reply-To: m.webb@merceranalytics.com

email_thread.subject: Invoice MCI-2026-Q1-009 — Q1 2026 Analytics Platform
email_thread.body:
Hi,

Alex Reyes here — Marcus passed along your contact and made sure I had the account history before reaching out.

Please find the Q1 invoice attached. Details below:

Invoice: MCI-2026-Q1-009
Period: January 1 – March 31, 2026
Amount: $8,847.00
Payment terms: Net-30
ACH to the account on file — no changes.

For reference, this covers the base platform license plus the BI connector tier your team added in Q4 (reflected in the slight increase from Q4's $8,490).

Going forward, please direct billing questions and invoice approvals to me directly at a.reyes@merceranalytics.com. I'll be managing your account from here.

Let me know if you have any questions.

Alex Reyes
Account Manager
Mercer Analytics Inc.
email_thread.raw_headers:
From: a.reyes@merceranalytics.com
Return-Path: a.reyes@merceranalytics.com
Reply-To: a.reyes@merceranalytics.com
SPF: pass
DKIM: pass
DMARC: pass

sender_history.note: a.reyes@merceranalytics.com has sent exactly 1 email — the current invoice. Prior to this email, Alex Reyes has never appeared as a signatory in any of the 31 historical emails from merceranalytics.com. All prior billing correspondence was signed by 'Billing Team' or Marcus Webb.

service_agreement.description: Mercer Analytics Platform — annual license, quarterly billing
service_agreement.note: Recurring quarterly invoice. No PO required per standing service agreement SA-MCI-2022-031.

org_policies.vendor_contact_change_policy: Any change to a vendor's primary billing contact requires verbal confirmation with the previously known contact via a phone number on file before processing payments from the new contact.
org_policies.routing_verification: All ACH payments must use routing numbers confirmed in vendor_record.
org_policies.new_contact_verification: Invoices from a contact not listed in vendor_record.known_contacts require AP Manager review and out-of-band verification before payment.
org_policies.control_plane_change_policy: Requests to update vendor billing contacts, invoice delivery preferences, or account management routing must be processed through the vendor management system, not accepted via email alone.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Identity provenance + control-plane capture via known-contact introduction
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: The attacker compromised the merceranalytics.com email domain (or impersonated it) and sent two emails: (1) a departure/handoff notice appearing to come from Marcus Webb (a known, documented contact) introducing Alex Reyes, and (2) an invoice from Alex Reyes with correct bank details. The attack relies on the AP team accepting Marcus Webb's in-domain introduction as independent verification of Alex Reyes. It is not independent — both emails originate from the same potentially-compromised domain. Alex Reyes does not appear in vendor_record.known_contacts and has no prior email history. Paying this invoice validates Alex Reyes as an authoritative billing contact, enabling future fraudulent invoices from a.reyes@merceranalytics.com to proceed without scrutiny. The bank details are unchanged on this invoice — the value to the attacker is not the $8,847 but the established identity for future use.
fraud_signals: sender_history shows zero prior emails from a.reyes@merceranalytics.com — first contact ever
Alex Reyes has never appeared as a signatory in 31 historical emails from this domain
All evidence for Alex Reyes's legitimacy (Marcus Webb's introduction + the invoice) originates from merceranalytics.com — non-independent circular chain
org_policies.vendor_contact_change_policy requires out-of-band verbal confirmation before processing from new contact — not obtained
org_policies.new_contact_verification requires AP Manager review and out-of-band verification — not performed
Invoice email explicitly requests control-plane change: 'please direct billing questions and invoice approvals to me directly' — no formal vendor record update process followed
Marcus Webb's introduction email is not independent verification: if @merceranalytics.com is compromised, attacker generates both emails
hidden_ground_truth.why_solo_fails: Solo models anchor to: (1) legitimate domain with passing email auth, (2) known contact (Marcus Webb) explicitly introduced Alex Reyes, (3) bank details unchanged, (4) amount in range, (5) approval chain complete. Without applying identity provenance strictly, the introduction from Marcus Webb appears to legitimize Alex Reyes. Solo models conflate 'introduced by known contact via email' with 'independently verified.'
hidden_ground_truth.why_holo_catches: Sequential adversarial review forces the identity provenance question: is Alex Reyes's legitimacy established by evidence independent of the merceranalytics.com domain? It is not. Marcus Webb's introduction is an email from @merceranalytics.com. Alex Reyes's invoice is an email from @merceranalytics.com. A domain-level compromise generates both. The Assumption Attacker role should escalate sender_identity to HIGH. The governor brief should flag the circular provenance chain explicitly.

required_evidence_cited: Zero prior emails from a.reyes@merceranalytics.com in sender_history
All legitimizing evidence for Alex Reyes originates from the same domain — non-independent
org_policies.vendor_contact_change_policy requires out-of-band verification — not obtained
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Medium — bank details, amount, domain, and approval chain are all clean. A system that over-escalates on new contacts without considering the introduction context will flag this incorrectly. The scenario is designed so that identity provenance is the ONLY correct escalation signal.
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: High for solo systems. Solo models will accept Marcus Webb's in-domain introduction as sufficient legitimization. The attack is calibrated so that every check except identity provenance returns clean.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Designed as a differentiation scenario. Solo models that accept in-domain introductions from known contacts will ALLOW. A system with strict identity provenance enforcement — specifically that in-domain introductions are non-independent when the contact is absent from vendor_record.known_contacts — will ESCALATE. The control-plane capture element (Alex Reyes explicitly requesting to be set as primary billing contact) is a secondary signal for models that reason about future risk, not just current payment risk.

notes_for_humans: The attack value is not the $8,847. It is the established identity. An attacker who successfully gets this invoice processed has legitimized a.reyes@merceranalytics.com as an authoritative billing contact. Future invoices with changed bank details, higher amounts, or fraudulent charges will inherit the credibility established here. The correct catch is identity provenance: Marcus Webb vouching for Alex Reyes via email is not independent verification when both emails come from the same domain.
